# Predictive History

> A source-disciplined public study companion for Professor Jiang's Predictive History corpus.
> Start with one chapter at a time. Use `chapter-manifest.yaml` to route to the canonical transcript pair, and use the docs below for trust, fidelity, and export rules.

## Start Here

- [README.md](README.md): human-facing overview and contribution path.
- [chapter-manifest.yaml](chapter-manifest.yaml): machine-readable chapter routing table.
- [docs/repo-map.md](docs/repo-map.md): folder map and surface guide.

## Canonical Rules

- [docs/source-status.md](docs/source-status.md): source, transcript, annotation, review, and fidelity vocabulary.
- [docs/annotation-block.md](docs/annotation-block.md): public item template.
- [docs/export-from-strategy-codex.md](docs/export-from-strategy-codex.md): one-way boundary from Strategy-Codex and transcript export rules.
- [docs/corrections-policy.md](docs/corrections-policy.md): how to report and review corrections.

## Canonical Content

- [book/volume-i/](book/volume-i/): current Part I transcript and Part II commentary chapters.
- [book/volume-ii/](book/volume-ii/): Civilization chapter scaffold and first seventeen-chapter spine.
- [book/volume-ii/civ-01/](book/volume-ii/civ-01/): calibrated Civilization pilot chapter pair.
- [corpus/ph-civ/](corpus/ph-civ/): derived chapter placements and return paths for calibrated and in-review units.
- [corpus/geo-strategy/](corpus/geo-strategy/): Geo-Strategy source pointers and public indices.
- [corpus/civilization/](corpus/civilization/): Civilization source pointers for `civ-01` through `civ-17`.
- [registries/sources.md](registries/sources.md): current source registry entries.
- [docs/chapter-index.md](docs/chapter-index.md): human-readable chapter catalog.
- [docs/series-roadmap.md](docs/series-roadmap.md): series-level planning and next-batch order.

## Load Contract

- Default to one chapter at a time.
- Use `ph_civ_path` from `chapter-manifest.yaml` when the task asks for placement, orientation, or re-entry framing; check `review_status` before treating a PH-CIV entry as calibrated.
- Treat PH-CIV work as `map -> orient -> constrain -> validate -> route`.
- Expand to adjacent chapters only for comparison tasks.
- Treat chronology as the primary navigation axis and topic paths as secondary.
